itemUpgrade.name: Balanced
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls made with this weapon.

itemUpgrade.name: Critical: Sharpened

item: melee weapons that deal piercing or slashing damage only

entries: Attacks with this weapon score a critical hit on a roll one lower than normal. For example, if you normally score a critical hit on a roll of 20 you instead score one on a roll of 19 or 20.

itemUpgrade.name: Critical: Sight Pin
entries: Attacks with this weapon score a critical hit on a roll one lower than normal. For example, if you normally score a critical hit on a roll of 20 you instead score one on a roll of 19 or 20.

itemUpgrade.name: Critical: Spiked

item: melee weapons that deal bludgeoning damage only

entries: Attacks with this weapon score a critical hit on a roll one lower than normal. For example, if you normally score a critical hit on a roll of 20 you instead score one on a roll of 19 or 20.

itemUpgrade.name: Gem Socket
entries: This weapon can be imbued with a gemstone.

itemUpgrade.name: Silvered
entries: Attacks with this weapon count as silvered for the purposes overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

itemUpgrade.name: Wounding: Keen
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to damage rolls made with this weapon.

itemUpgrade.name: Wounding: Oiled String
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to damage rolls made with this weapon.

itemUpgrade.name: Brutal
entries: When you attack with this weapon and roll the maximum result for the weapon's damage dice, you can roll these dice again and add the new roll to the damage of the attack. If you roll the maximum amount again, you can repeat this process until you don't.

itemUpgrade.name: Enchanted

item: any 1st tier tag, can only be applied by an arcane spellcaster, quarterstaffs only

entries: You gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls while using this weapon as a spellcasting focus.

itemUpgrade.name: Flanged

item: spiked tag, can only be applied to maces and mauls

entries: When you hit a creature wearing medium or heavy armor with this weapon you sunder its armor, inflicting a cumulative -1 penalty to its Armor Class until the armor is repaired.

itemUpgrade.name: Magical

item: silvered tag, can only be applied by an arcane spellcaster

entries: Attacks with this weapon count as magical for the purposes of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage, and features such as {@creature black pudding}'s {@i corrosive form}.

itemUpgrade.name: Saw-toothed

item: sharpened tag, can only be applied to daggers

entries: Attacks with this weapon deal an extra {@dice 1d4} slashing damage. No effect against constructs or undead.

itemUpgrade.name: Superior

item: balanced, keen, or oiled string tag, can only be applied to weapons with one damage die

entries: The damage die of this weapon is increased by one size (for example, {@dice 1d6} becomes {@dice 1d8}, and {@dice 1d10} becomes {@dice 1d12}) to a maximum of {@dice 1d12}.

itemUpgrade.name: Arcane

item: enchanted tag, can only be applied by an arcane spellcaster

entries: You gain a +1 bonus to your spell save DC while using this weapon as a spellcasting focus.

itemUpgrade.name: Masterwork
entries: You gain an additional +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon.

itemUpgrade.name: Armor Proofing: 1st Tier
entries: While wearing this armor, if you would take 6 or less nonmagical slashing damage before resistance is applied, you take none of that damage instead.

itemUpgrade.name: Armor Proofing: 2nd Tier

item: medium or heavy armor with 1st tier armor proofing tag

entries: While wearing this armor, if you would take 7 or less nonmagical slashing or piercing damage before resistance is applied, you take none of that damage instead.

itemUpgrade.name: Armor Proofing: 3rd Tier

item: heavy armor with the 2nd tier armor proofing tag

entries: While wearing this armor, if you would take 8 or less nonmagical slashing, piercing, or bludgeoning damage before resistance is applied, you take none of that damage instead.

itemUpgrade.name: Breathable

item: light or medium armor, incompatible with the insulated tag

entries: While wearing this armor, you have advantage on saving throws you make against exhaustion effects due to extreme heat, as described in {@book chapter 5|DMG|5} of the {@i Dungeon Master's Guide}.

itemUpgrade.name: Burnished
entries: This armor has been polished to a mirror finish. While wearing it, you may have advantage on Charisma-based skill checks when interacting with certain humanoids, as determined by the DM. This tag is automatically removed after 24 hours of wear or at the end of a combat.

itemUpgrade.name: Climbing Harness
entries: This armor has been modified with a climbing harness around the midriff, comprising leather straps and quickdraws. While wearing it, you make Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks to scale vertical surfaces with advantage when using a rope or similar aid.

itemUpgrade.name: Decorated

item: medium or heavy armor and shields

entries: This armor or shield is adorned with a holy symbol and can be used as a spellcasting focus for cleric and paladin spells.

itemUpgrade.name: Insulated
entries: This armor counts as cold weather gear in conditions of extreme cold, as described in {@book chapter 5|DMG|5} of the {@i Dungeon Master's Guide}.

itemUpgrade.name: Locking Joints
entries: This armor is made with hinged joints that can be locked by a quick motion from the wearer. While wearing it, you make Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks to oppose attempts to shove you with advantage.

itemUpgrade.name: Muffled
entries: This armor no longer confers disadvantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks.

itemUpgrade.name: Quick-release Clasps
entries: You can doff this armor as an action.

itemUpgrade.name: Reinforced
entries: This armor has been reinforced at the joints and other weak points. While wearing it, critical damage you take from nonmagical attacks is reduced by 3.

itemUpgrade.name: Gem Socket

item: armor proofing tag (any tier) or a shield

entries: This armor or shield can be imbued with a gemstone.

itemUpgrade.name: Spiked
entries: This armor has been modified with spikes, barbs, or other similar feature, and deals {@dice 1d4} piercing damage to creatures attacking with unarmed strikes or natural weapons that aren't magical.
